notes_md:
**dô** = “cheers!” — what you shout before drinking alcohol together.

It’s an exclamation, not a verb, so it usually stands alone and is said loudly in a group. Often everyone counts together, then shouts it at the same time.  


You’ll also see it spelled **dzô** or **zô** in casual writing — same sound, just playful spelling. Think of it as the Vietnamese version of “bottoms up!” 🍻
exampleSentencesTranslated: Every time friends gather, everyone shouts 'cheers' before drinking.
I propose this glass to you, cheers!
At the birthday party, the whole group said cheers very happily.
exampleSentences: Mỗi lần bạn bè tụ họp, mọi người đều hô lớn 'dô' trước khi uống.
Anh mời em ly này, dô nào!
Khi ăn tiệc sinh nhật, cả nhóm cùng nói dô rất vui vẻ.

content.sentence: Anh mời em ly này, ___ nào!

translation.en: I offer you this glass, cheers!

notes:
dô = “cheers!” — what you shout before drinking alcohol together.

It’s an exclamation, not a verb, so it usually stands alone and is said loudly in a group. Often everyone counts together, then shouts it at the same time.  

You’ll also see it spelled dzô or zô in casual writing — same sound, just playful spelling. Think of it as the Vietnamese version of “bottoms up!” 🍻
